title: CoAP client does not validate response token
description:
File: rust/lichen-coap/src/client.rs:134-161

The decode() function extracts the token from responses (line 141: tkl) but never compares it to the request token. RFC 7252 Section 5.3.1 states clients MUST match responses by token.

Current code:
    let tkl = (data[0] & 0x0f) as usize;
    // ... tkl used only for parsing, never validated against request token

Impact (SECURITY): An attacker on the local network could inject spoofed CoAP responses with arbitrary tokens, and the client would accept them as valid responses. This enables:
- Data injection attacks (fake sensor readings, configuration changes)
- Denial of service (causing client to process fake responses)
- Protocol confusion

Combined with the fixed 'LICH' token (see related issue), the attacker knows exactly what token to use.

Fix: Store the request token and verify the response token matches before processing.
close_reason: Fixed: Added token validation to decode() per RFC 7252 Section 5.3.1. The response token is now compared against the expected request token, rejecting spoofed responses with mismatched tokens. Added 5 unit tests covering matching tokens, mismatched tokens, wrong length tokens, and empty token edge cases.
